prompt: How many evaluations were submitted for each course?

inputData.question: How many evaluations were submitted for each course?

{"status":"success","steps":{"input":{"question":"How many evaluations were submitted for each course?"},"sql":{"payload":{"question":"How many evaluations were submitted for each course?"},"startedAt":1771855729196,"status":"success","output":{"reasoning":"The question asks for the count of evaluations submitted for each course. I need to join course_evaluations → enrollments → course_sections → courses to group by course and count evaluations. The path is: course_evaluations.enrollment_id → enrollments.id, enrollments.course_section_id → course_sections.id, course_sections.course_id → courses.id. I'll count distinct evaluation IDs per course and order by course code for deterministic output.","sql":"
SELECT 
  c.code AS course_code,
  c.name AS course_name,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS evaluation_count
FROM courses c
INNER JOIN course_sections cs ON c.id = cs.course_id
INNER JOIN enrollments e ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
INNER JOIN course_evaluations ce ON e.id = ce.enrollment_id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY c.code
","assumptions":["Evaluations are linked to courses through the enrollment → course_section → course chain","Each course_evaluation record represents one submitted evaluation","We count all evaluations regardless of submission date or rating values"]},"endedAt":1771855772354},"run-raw-sql":{"payload":{"sql":"

visualization.title: Evaluations Submitted per Course
visualization.description: Bar chart showing the number of course evaluations submitted for each course

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT 
  c.code AS course_code,
  c.name AS course_name,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS evaluation_count
FROM courses c
INNER JOIN course_sections cs ON c.id = cs.course_id
INNER JOIN enrollments e ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
INNER JOIN course_evaluations ce ON e.id = ce.enrollment_id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY c.code

visualization.title: Evaluations Submitted per Course
visualization.description: Bar chart showing the number of course evaluations submitted for each course

result.sql:
SELECT 
  c.code AS course_code,
  c.name AS course_name,
  COUNT(ce.id) AS evaluation_count
FROM courses c
INNER JOIN course_sections cs ON c.id = cs.course_id
INNER JOIN enrollments e ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
INNER JOIN course_evaluations ce ON e.id = ce.enrollment_id
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY c.code
